does: HealthBench introduces a comprehensive evaluation benchmark designed to assess AI models in realistic healthcare scenarios. Developed through collaboration with over 250 physicians, the benchmark provides standardized testing protocols and metrics for evaluating both performance and safety of AI systems deployed in clinical settings. Rather than assessing models on isolated medical datasets, HealthBench frames evaluation tasks within authentic healthcare workflows and decision-making contexts, capturing how AI systems actually interact with clinical practice and patient care. The benchmark includes multiple task modalities and clinical specialties, establishing baseline performance metrics across these diverse scenarios.
connects: This work builds on growing recognition within the AI and medical AI communities that existing evaluation methods often fail to capture real-world deployment challenges. It responds to fragmentation in healthcare AI evaluation, where diverse institutions have developed proprietary or specialty-specific benchmarks without a shared reference standard. HealthBench extends prior work on medical AI datasets and benchmarks by prioritizing clinical realism and safety considerations alongside accuracy, drawing from clinical validation methodologies traditionally used in healthcare research. The physician-collaborative design approach reflects recent trends emphasizing stakeholder involvement in AI system evaluation, particularly in high-stakes domains where model failures carry direct human consequences.
enables: HealthBench establishes infrastructure for comparative evaluation that could catalyze standardization in healthcare AI development and deployment. By providing a shared reference benchmark, it enables researchers and developers to measure progress consistently, identify systematic weaknesses in models across different clinical contexts, and prioritize safety improvements before real-world deployment. The benchmark framework can facilitate regulatory evaluation and approval processes by offering transparent, physician-validated assessment criteria. Future work can extend HealthBench across additional specialties, incorporate dynamic clinical scenarios, and develop interpretability evaluation methods that help clinicians understand AI system reasoning, ultimately supporting safer and more trustworthy AI integration into healthcare practice.
